title: Naphthoxyacetylacetophenone as a Selective Reagent for Determination of Iron
author: M. B. DESHMUKH
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Department of Chemistry, Nagpur University, Nagpur-440 010 

  Manuscript received 26 June 1984, revised 11 January 1985, accepted 27 April 1985  

 A new spectrophotometric method for microdetermination of iron using naphthoxy­acetylacetophenone (NAA) is reported. The complex is formed instantaneously in cold at pH 5.5 and can be extracted in toluene. It shows maximum absorbance at 390 nm. Beer's law is valid over the range of 0.5 to 8.0  µ g Fe ml  -1  . The complex is stable and sensitivity of determination is 0.008  µ g Fe cm -2 . Many common ions are tolerated to a large extent. The method is simple, rapid, selective and can be used satisfactorily for the determination of iron in drug and ore.
